Logic Analysis: config.py
Contains application configuration class (Config, DevelopmentConfig, ProductionConfig) and database URI setup. No dependencies on other project files.
models.py
Contains SQLAlchemy ORM models: User, Transaction, AuthenticationAttempt, AuditLog, FlaggedTransaction. Depends on flask_sqlalchemy, config.py for database connection.
utils.py
Contains utility functions like token generation, validation helpers, and date formatting. Depends on flask, jwt_extended, dateutil.
services/__init__.py
Empty init file for services package. No dependencies.
services/auth_service.py
Contains AuthService class with methods: authenticate, lock_account, unlock_account, get_failed_attempts. Depends on models.py (User, AuthenticationAttempt), utils.py.
services/audit_service.py
Contains AuditService class with methods: log_action, get_audit_logs. Depends on models.py (AuditLog).
services/fraud_service.py

Contains FraudDetectionService class with rule-based heuristics methods: analyze_transaction (rapid withdrawals, near-limit amounts, unusual hours), flag_transaction, get_flagged_transactions. Depends on models.py (Transaction, FlaggedTransaction), services/audit_service.py.
services/transaction_service.py

Contains TransactionService class with methods: process_withdrawal, check_balance_and_limit, execute_withdrawal, rollback. Depends on models.py (User, Transaction), services/fraud_service.py, services/audit_service.py.
services/admin_service.py
Contains AdminService class with methods: review_flagged_transaction, lock_unlock_account, get_transaction_history, get_audit_logs_history. Depends on models.py, services/audit_service.py.
api/__init__.py
Empty init file for API package. No dependencies.
api/auth_api.py
Contains Flask-RESTful resource AuthLogin (POST /auth/login), AuthLogout. Depends on flask_restful, services/auth_service.py, services/audit_service.py, utils.py.
api/transaction_api.py
Contains Flask-RESTful resource Withdrawal (POST /withdraw), TransactionHistory (GET /history). Depends on flask_restful, services/transaction_service.py, utils.py.
api/admin_api.py

Contains Flask-RESTful resources: FlaggedList (GET /admin/flagged), ReviewFlagged (POST /admin/review), AccountLock (POST /admin/account/lock). Depends on flask_restful, services/admin_service.py, services/fraud_service.py, utils.py.
main.py
Application entry point: creates Flask app, registers blueprints, initializes database and migration, runs server. Depends on all modules above for imports and initialization.
Full API spec:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: ATM Withdrawal System API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /auth/login:
    post:
      summary: Authenticate user with card number and PIN
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                card_number:
                  type: string
                pin:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Authentication successful, returns JWT token
        '401':
          description: Invalid credentials
        '423':
          description: Account is locked due to failed attempts
  /withdraw:
    post:
      summary: Process a withdrawal transaction
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                amount:
                  type: number
                  format: float
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Withdrawal successful, returns transaction ID
        '400':
          description: Invalid amount or exceeds limits
        '401':
          description: Unauthorized (invalid/expired token)
  /history:
    get:
      summary: Get user's transaction history
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Returns list of transactions
  /admin/flagged:
    get:
      summary: Get all flagged transactions (admin only)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Returns list of flagged transactions
  /admin/review:
    post:
      summary: Review a flagged transaction (admin only)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                flagged_id:
                  type: integer
                status:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approved, declined]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Review status updated
  /admin/account/lock:
    post:
      summary: Lock or unlock a user account (admin only)
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                user_id:
                  type: integer
                lock:
                  type: boolean
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Account lock status updated
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
Shared Knowledge: `config.py` contains all configuration variables like SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URI, FRAUD_THRESHOLDS (time between withdrawals, daily limit percentages, unusual hours). `utils.py` provides helper functions for JWT token creation/validation, PIN hashing with bcrypt, and date/time parsing. All services use SQLAlchemy sessions obtained from `current_app.extensions['sqlalchemy'].session`. Audit logging is done asynchronously within service methods to ensure non-blocking operations.
Anything UNCLEAR: Clarification needed on whether the ATM terminal integration will use RESTful HTTPS directly or require an intermediary middleware. Also need confirmation on the specific rule thresholds for fraud detection (e.g., time window for rapid withdrawals, percentage of daily limit threshold, hours considered unusual). The current design assumes configurable constants that can be set in config.py.
